title: Leontodon radiatus
intro: Leontodon radiatus, also known as the radiate hawk's beard or yellow hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, and its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating gastrointestinal issues. Leontodon radiatus contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones (e.g., leontalactone and leontodin), flavonoids (e.g., quercetin and kaempferol), and phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic and ferulic acid), which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Leontodon radiatus is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as colds, bronchitis, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Leontodon radiatus is classified as a species of dandelion-like plants within the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and shared traits with other species in the same family. Specifically, it belongs to the genus Leontodon and the order Asterales. The species is further classified as a perennial herb with a specific growth habit and leaf structure. Its classification is an essential aspect of its identification and study in the field of botany.
